title: SPD PAL Program -- $50K DCJS Anti-Violence Grant
summary: Deputy Chief Tom Series presents item 39, a $50,000 grant from NYS DCJS Anti-Violence Initiative for the Police Athletic League. Funding covers equipment, uniforms, field trips, a teens-and-cops podcast series, a barbershop skills program for non-traditional career paths, and CPR/first aid certification for youth.
raw_content:
[01:15:26] item 39.
[01:15:28] >> Good afternoon, counselors. Uh Deputy
[01:15:30] Chief [clears throat] Tom Series,
[01:15:31] please. I have item 39 is to enter into
[01:15:34] agreement with New York State Department
[01:15:35] of Criminal Justice Services for a uh
[01:15:37] $50,000 grant from their anti-violence
[01:15:40] initiative program to assist with our
[01:15:42] PAL program. The funding will go towards
[01:15:45] purchasing equipment, uniforms,
[01:15:46] conducting field trips. Uh,
[01:15:48] additionally, we're looking at um
[01:15:51] hosting a
[01:15:53] teens and cops podcast series. Um, a
[01:15:58] barbershop
[01:16:00] program for teens to develop additional
[01:16:02] skills if they're not going towards the
[01:16:04] traditional schooling route and also
[01:16:06] work towards getting them CPR first aid
[01:16:09] certified.
[01:16:13] >> Any questions?
[01:16:16] >> Okay. Thank you.
processing_notes: IPS detection is false positive (from 'tips' in 'field trips'). PAL = Police Athletic League. DCJS = Division of Criminal Justice Services.
Segment 15 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
